claimText: The Commission for Air Quality Management (CAQM) is a statutory body functioning under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
evidenceExcerpt: Statutory body under Commission for Air Quality Management in National Capital Region and Adjoining Areas Act 2021. Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
examUse: Statement-based question on the nature and nodal ministry of CAQM.

claimText: The jurisdiction of CAQM covers the National Capital Region (NCR) and adjoining areas in the states of Haryana, Punjab, Rajasthan, and Uttar Pradesh.
evidenceExcerpt: Protects and improves air quality in NCR and Adjoining Areas (Haryana, Punjab, Rajasthan and Uttar Pradesh).
examUse: Statement-based question on the territorial jurisdiction of CAQM.

claimText: In case of a conflict, the orders of the CAQM override those of state governments, the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB), and state Pollution Control Boards (PCBs).
evidenceExcerpt: Its orders override those of state governments, CPCB, state PCBs, and others in case of conflict.
examUse: Statement-based question on the overriding powers and hierarchy of CAQM versus CPCB and State PCBs.
distractorHooks: State government orders prevail in state territory

claimText: The Chairperson of the CAQM must possess at least 15 years of experience in environment protection or 25 years of experience in administration.
evidenceExcerpt: Chairperson must have at least 15 years in environment protection or 25 years in administration.
examUse: Statement-based question on the eligibility criteria for the CAQM Chairperson.
distractorHooks: Must be a retired Supreme Court judge
Requires 10 years of experience in environment protection
